observations.summary: Dan experiences a profound emotional reaction to the vision of a warm, ordinary, and unhaunted family, which contrasts sharply with his cold and hard reality.

evidence.quote: Dan wavered. The vision of it—a whole, unhaunted family, warm and ordinary and unhaunted—struck him like a blade of light against everything cold and hard in his reality.

observations.notes: This observation highlights the central emotional conflict experienced by Dan, emphasizing the contrast between his desires and his current situation.

observations.summary: The hunger Dan feels for the vision is described as a hole that penetrates deeply, symbolizing an intense longing that reaches to the other side of the world.

evidence.quote: The hunger of it was a hole that went straight through to the other side of the world.

observations.notes: This metaphor conveys the depth and intensity of Dan's yearning, suggesting it is all-consuming and far-reaching.
